title: Highly Recommend Stepful’s CCMA Program for a Strong Start in Healthcare
body:
I had a fantastic experience with Stepful’s CCMA program and would absolutely recommend it to anyone considering a career as a medical assistant. From start to finish, the program was well-structured, supportive, and designed with real student success in mind.

The coursework was clear, engaging, and practical, making it easy to understand even the more complex topics. I especially appreciated how the program balanced flexibility with accountability—it allowed me to learn at my own pace while still staying on track. The instructors and support staff were incredibly responsive and genuinely invested in helping students succeed, which made a huge difference in my confidence throughout the program.

One of the standout aspects for me was how career-focused the training was. The CCMA program didn’t just prepare me to pass an exam—it helped me feel ready to step into a real clinical environment. From hands-on knowledge to professional guidance, I felt fully supported every step of the way.

Overall, Stepful exceeded my expectations. If you’re looking for a program that is accessible, thorough, and truly committed to your success, I highly recommend Stepful’s CCMA program.

body:
I absolutely loved Miss L.. I have been to school more than once and the patients that woman has is remarkable. Couldn’t have asked for a better person to teach all levels of learning. So thank you to her!

My biggest issue would be the communication between my coach and I. I still to this day have no idea who they were. I would send an email and/or text and receive answers from different respondents. That was tough. I felt like I needed to explain a question again to the next person. Also, I’d ask a question through the classroom and it would just take way too long for a response.

There was confusion around externship hours. I was told I needed the 80 hours of externship as it is part of the program and I wouldn’t be finished with the program until those were completed. I had a lot of stress around this as I couldn’t find a site. Once I found out the hrs were for my benefit and experience and I had already graduated the program regardless of the hours, I secured a job with home health right away. But there was definitely a good gap of time I wasted stressing on this.
Hope this helps.

title: I completed my externship through…
body:
I completed my externship through Stepful at Richmond Foot & Ankle Clinic, but my experience was not handled professionally. My externship hours and work were not acknowledged, even though I provided proper documentation with staff signatures. I also experienced wage discrepancies and missing hours during my employment at the affiliated clinic. The clinic wdita your hours on the time clock. And the clock shows edited by The owner. In order to evade paying her employees their entire check. For WORKED WAGES! When I presented documentation showing the correct hours, staff AND STEPFUL dismissed it and claimed it was untrue, despite their own signatures being on the forms. My goal was to complete my training and contribute to the clinic, but the lack of honesty, accountability, and respect made the experience extremely disappointing. I hope future students and employees receive better treatment and transparency.
During my time there, I also experienced wage discrepancies. Hours I worked were removed from my paycheck, and when I presented accurate records, staff claimed the documentation was “a lie,” even though their signatures were clearly on the forms.
This created an unsafe and unprofessional learning environment. Students and employees deserve transparency, respect, and accurate record‑keeping. My experience did not reflect that, and I hope Stepful reevaluates this site as an externship placement.I also dealt with SEXUAL HARRASMENT BY THE DOCTORS BOYFRIEND. THAT WAS DISMISSED AS WELL.
This experience showed a serious lack of integrity and accountability. I would not recommend this site for externships or employment until major changes are made. AND AT THIS TIME I NO LONGER RECOMMEND STEPFUL AS A COMPANY TO BE TRUSTED.

title: Student-Stepful review
body: My journey with Stepful was overall a great experience. I’m grateful for the education, graduating, and securing my externship. The program gave me a strong foundation and helped me move forward in my career. One area I believe could be improved is the limited timeframe for career support after graduation. The window for job placement assistance felt shorter than expected, especially in a competitive healthcare job market where finding the right position can take time. Extending or offering more flexible access to career services would better support graduates during their transition into the workforce. Overall, I appreciate the experience, but longer term career support would make the program even stronger.

title: Review for Stepful (CPhT) Program
body:
I attended Stepful to become a Pharmacy Technician, and the experience was genuinely positive. The Zoom classes were very helpful, and Mrs. Castro was hands down an amazing instructor—she made learning fun, engaging, and easy to understand. The classes never felt intimidating; it was a safe space to ask questions and actually learn.

I graduated from the program with an A+ and was one of the top students in my class. I’m now officially a Certified Pharmacy Technician. The overall process was smooth, and everything came together the way it was supposed to. If you apply yourself and stay consistent, this program is absolutely doable.

title: My experience with Stepful has been…
body:
My experience with Stepful has been truly great and rewarding. The program is well-structured, easy to follow, and designed to help students succeed from day one. The instructors are knowledgeable, supportive, and take the time to explain concepts clearly, making even challenging material feel manageable. I also appreciated how encouraging and responsive the support team was—they genuinely want to see you succeed.

What made my experience stand out most was the balance between learning the technical skills needed to become a pharmacy technician and building confidence to enter the healthcare field. Stepful provided a supportive learning environment, practical training, and clear guidance toward certification and career readiness. I feel more prepared, motivated, and confident about my future because of this program, and I would highly recommend Stepful to anyone looking to start a meaningful career in healthcare.

title: Complete Fraud and Deception - Do Not Trust This Company!
body:
I was a student in Stepful's Clinical Medical Assistant course and was completely deceived. The Enrollment Agreement promised 3 hours per day of instruction, but the company delivered a fraudulent product. We only had two live classes per week that were only 1.5 hours long, plus recorded videos that were less than 30 minutes a day. The quality was terrible, with repetitive content and an unprofessional instructor.

When I tried to cancel, they accepted my request but refused to process the partial refund I am owed under my contract. They are not only deceptive but also refuse to honor their own legally binding agreement.

Poor communication. I waited for more than a week for someone to contact me to clarify the next steps for cancellation and refund. I sent countless emails and they didn't care at all. It's unacceptable that a school in the healthcare field is still operating this way.

Save yourself the trouble and avoid this school at all costs.

I was asked to go in for a drug test, not once but twice and to upload documents twice as well for the Georgia board of pharmacy. Because I was not able to get my license in time they didn't give me a job placement and didn't give me a refund which I am furious about. When also taking the pharmacy technician certification exam, I did it online through the national health accreditation website as Stepful had advised me to do, yet failed twice due to technical issues on their end. I mean why would the NHA allow people to schedule to take the exam on a day your website is going to be "under maintenance"? | would like a refund, after all I didn't get the job placement as advertised despite participating, paying the tuition in full and had technical issues that were not on my end.

title: My Stepful Experience
body:
In short, all the information is on PowerPoint slides from a book. The zoom lectures are the teachers just reading off the PowerPoints and telling the students about their experiences.

There are some "labs" where you practice, but it feels like a game. In theory, it sounds good, but there's nothing like hands on experience. My suggestion (I got from a classmate) is to volunteer at a private clinic and to let them know of your future intentions.

But, remember, this is an online course, thus most of what you learn is just not super useful to be applied if your work is more hands on by definition. No one is communicative in terms of your externship. Honestly, after being met with rudeness or just flat out nothing at all, I would never recommend this company. The phone number doesn’t even work to call them on their website. They claim to be helpful in getting you a career and help you professionally edit your resume, but even the skills they put on the resume are things I am not permitted to do within my scope of practice without getting in tons of trouble. It’s ridiculous. The only other benefit aside from me getting my certification is that this environment is very inclusive and diverse, promoting that heavily, which I appreciate.

title: Stepful needs work…
body:
I am a recent graduate from Stepful’s Pharmacy Technician program. When first enrolling, I was very impressed with the school and the customer service I was receiving. However, as time went on, I started to notice a few “red flags.”

I was assigned a coach at the beginning of my program, which I quickly realized was just automated messages and were not catered or personalized to me in anyway. However, I do believe a real person would respond if you emailed them back with a question or concern. The reply back I usually received however, never really answered my questions, so take that however you will. My “coach” also changed many times throughout my four month program. Not sure if the company has a high turnover rate or what.

The program material itself was okay. Nothing spectacular, had a few typos throughout, but taught me what I needed to know, nonetheless.

The externship portion is a complete joke. If you’re interested in a hospital externship, don’t fall for their false promises like I did. I was told throughout the entire program of my interest was to do my unpaid externship in a hospital, it could be done and my coach and coordinator would accommodate that. It wasn’t until the last week or so of class that I was told Stepful ONLY has retail pharmacy partnerships. Also, I wasn’t going to be placed at the retail pharmacy until 2-3 months AFTER graduation. Ultimately, I made the decision to opt out, which forfeits my eligibility for job placement and their “money back guaranteed” contact if I am unable to find a job.

Post graduation, it was nearly impossible to get any response from Stepful. I tried multiple emails, chatted in using their chat box, and even called. It took about a week after I passed my NHA to get in contact with someone to release my certification. I lost count at how many attempts I made to have it released before someone finally got back to me.

Overall, I think there are better options out there. The only thing that sets Stepful apart from any other online program is the weekly live lectures. Stepful got me the certification I needed, but the experience was rather lackluster and gave me quite a few headaches.

title: Ehh…
body: Overall, you get what you put in. My first teacher did not teach at allll; she spent the time just chatting with students and often spoke religiously saying things like “I pray to God y’all will do well .. I hope God watches over you..” which I found inappropriate. Thankfully she ended up leaving due to an “emergency” and the second teacher was so much more helpful. However, majority of the learning will be done from teaching yourself. Class is disruptive and there is no format to class time. They put you in random groups with other people that don’t have similar schedules as you or are in different time zones, making it hard to meet. Externship coaches lack communication. I have classmates that have been waiting OVER two months to be placed for an externship and haven’t, so they can’t be fully certified because of Stepful’s negligence. The only upside is that you can take the class online and pay in installments.

title: Don't waste your money.
body: I hate to say this because my instructor was amazing, the whole class was interactive with one another, and overall I had a great experience. I passed the Pharmacy Technician classes and was so hopeful because I kickstarted my future. When you start your externship- it takes them weeks to find a placement... even if it's in your hometown. Not only that but after you begin, there is a daily log you keep to track your work on the website. The site will crash, and lose all your data now and then. So, if you do use this program, write your hours down. I was asked by multiple different Stepful employees if I had started my externship after I had already finished. There is no communication with staff unless you make the first call or email. And get this, every time I contacted my externship coordinator who I could ask all my questions, I was emailed back by someone different every single time. Classes were fine but felt boring because the Zooms were so long and frequent, but that may just be me having a Kinesthetic learning style. It was also hard to reach instructors at times, even though they had just gotten off a lecture. If you're looking into being a Pharmacy Technician, I'd go to your local CVS or Walgreens- they're also accredited and can push you in the right direction to pass your exam. You get paid to train and usually, if you sign a contract to work at said business for a few years, they'll also pay for your test! I wish I would've known that!

title: Be Diligent
body:
The Internship was great and most of the coaches and teachers were fantastic. My advice is to apply for jobs even if you feel you are under qualified. Also, if you participate in everything, including the extra credit. that I feel is where you learn the most. The extra credit gives you some hands on experience even if it is with dummies. Also practice taking blood pressure on yourself and friends or family. Be diligent in everything!

The one down side is that I notice Stepful says a lot of what is not actually true and they find so many loop holes to get away with it. Keep the e-mail that says you will receive a refund if you do not find a job within 6 months after you graduate since that changed to after completing the job readiness cohort for me. Some of the coaches also blew you off or were very rude. Always wait to hear back from your main coach or keep all e-mails from them if they are coming off as unprofessional.

title: The career coaches after the Stepful…
body:
The career coaches after the Stepful program that help in job searches are amazing. Specifically, Crystal Rubin and Tracye Warfield. They encourage you and give you so much constructive criticism, allowing you to be prepared when applying to jobs and going through the interview process. They have a wealth of knowledge from years of experience, giving you resources not only to use now in your job search but to aid you in the future.

The externship coaches were not as helpful and were lacking in communication both with my externship site and me, the student.

The program does prepare you for success; you just have to get through it and pass the certification exam.

Some of the professors are good at teaching the material and giving real-life examples, while effectively using the time in class. Other professors are not punctual and reiterate topics that should be addressed in an email or after class, not taking up 30 minutes of the class before we breach the content intended for the lecture.

title: I’ll give and update review once I…
body: I’ll give and update review once I graduate the program on April 4 and I’m only leaving a review because I know it’s helpful for some . I was scared as shit before starting the program I kept contemplating bc I didn’t want to get scammed out of 1100. I did reviews for month and kept coming back to the website just checking periodically. When I initially was about to sign up I was so scared and hesitant that I let the class get full bc of my procrastination lol something told me to call and ask if someone drops the class before it began do an open spot bc available again and I kept checking the website kept checking the website until I saw an opening again . Once a spot became available I applied for the program still nervous ! Began Jan 23,2025 and will be don’t April 4, 2025 . And I’m glad i made that decision . I haven’t encounter any problem with the program the material or the teacher so so far so good for me .

title: Solid 4

title: The department you have to make sure…
body: The department you have to make sure that the answers to the texts and quizzes is correct is terrible!! It’s hard enough for me to learn pharmacy math and you’re making it more difficult by not having the correct answers. It’s making it harder for our instructor as well. Please double check your answers to the calculations BEFORE you upload the tests, quizzes, so we can know that the formulas you’re teaching is accurate. Wrong answers do not help when you already struggle with math problems.

They want to help you in every way possible, and that's what was so great about this program. They're very kind and understanding.

title: Changed my life
body: Stepful helped me completely reimagine my life. The dedicated staff helped me when I was in and out of the hospital and struggling with finances and classes, helping me find grants and alternatives to make my certification affordable and keep me on track when life was getting the better of me. The instructors stayed in calls for one on one support while I was in my hospital bed until almost midnight to make sure I didn't just pass and meet a quota but graduate with a 92 and become certified with a 91 on the NHA exam. Even after, they helped me overhaul my resume and entire perspective to land a full time job in the medical field with incredible benefits. Stepful has been one of the best educational experiences I've ever had with a community of alumni and staff supporting each other every step of the way.

title: 4out of 5 – The program felt very rushed with a lot of work in a short amount of time, and it was and the price was somewhat difficult for my budget.
body:
5 out of 5 stars
I would give Stepful 5 stars because the program is well-organized, supportive, and easy to follow. The instructors explain concepts clearly, the learning materials are helpful, and the hands-on practice prepares students for real clinical settings. Overall, it’s a great program for anyone pursuing a career as a medical assistant.
